---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 0ce3ef20dff08cd6a6a23b1701c81d691339fc4a
findings:
  - { id: judged-f1, kind: judged, text: "dc-2 places StatusGroupActiveComponents — still-ACTIVE components — into the 'settling' bucket, but parent dc-4 defines the trailing settling group as 'any remaining active statuses (closed awaiting archive, superseded)', an enumeration that names only wind-down statuses. Classifying a component that is still active and presumably still actionable under the bucket the parent frames as post-actionable residue is at best an undeclared gap-fill and at worst a contradiction of dc-4's actionable-first taxonomy. The cited adjudication ADJ-32 settled only zone-aware exclusion of archive-zone Terminal entries; no adjudication, supersedes, or exempts edge is declared for the ActiveComponents→settling placement specifically. (confidence 0.45)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-36 (2026-07-16): parent dc-4's 'any remaining active statuses' is a TOTAL catch-all over the three-bucket partition's remainder (the author of the ADJ-24 taxonomy is the sitting adjudicator); the parenthetical '(closed awaiting archive, superseded)' illustrates the common wind-down cases and does not bound it. A still-active component is neither awaiting design/accept action nor in the build queue, so the trailing group is its only home; excluding it would make the glance a partial answer to parent dc-2's 'what state is everything in'. No conflict. The finding's semantic point is met at the presentation layer: the trailing group's display label may be worded honestly for steady-state entries at build discretion, within dc-5's fixed glance-group-settling testid (testids address, they do not dictate display text).", target_ref: spec/workbench-legibility }
  - { id: judged-f2, kind: judged, text: "dc-3 grants matrix and verdict links only to a default-branch class:feature entry with a non-empty story field ('today's exact condition'), while parent dc-4 promises 'matrix and verdict additionally for features' without qualification. The ADJ-32/f3 adjudication dc-3 cites covers the still-drafting feature on a design branch (no built evidence to link); it does not cover the residual case of a default-branch feature whose story field is empty — such an entry loses the matrix/verdict links the parent promises, and no supersedes/exempts edge or adjudication is declared for that narrowing. (confidence 0.40)", disposition: rejected, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-36 (2026-07-16) confirms rejected per ADJ-32: the original f3 explicitly enumerated 'a default-branch feature with no stories yet, gets a board link only' and ADJ-32 rejected f3 in full. A default-branch feature with an empty story field has no story ref to construct a /matrix or /verdict address — the link cannot be built, and dc-3 mirrors directory.go's existing condition (class is feature and the story field is non-empty; dc-1 consumed-not-re-derived, never a third grammar): a link that cannot work does not exist to give.", target_ref: spec/workbench-legibility }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/home-status-glance#dc-1, spec/home-status-glance#dc-2, spec/home-status-glance#dc-3, spec/home-status-glance#dc-4, spec/home-status-glance#dc-5, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-1, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-2, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-3, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-4, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:de3110614890bf1c83011d47a7e198f0fe2c8b01206882181704d3e84f2f8712
integrity: sha256:8ffc971d466c327a521ea9abbfa03faee910fa285be01e0459ed765514af8db8
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"f1\",\"text\":\"dc-2 places StatusGroupActiveComponents — still-ACTIVE components — into the 'settling' bucket, but parent dc-4 defines the trailing settling group as 'any remaining active statuses (closed awaiting archive, superseded)', an enumeration that names only wind-down statuses. Classifying a component that is still active and presumably still actionable under the bucket the parent frames as post-actionable residue is at best an undeclared gap-fill and at worst a contradiction of dc-4's actionable-first taxonomy. The cited adjudication ADJ-32 settled only zone-aware exclusion of archive-zone Terminal entries; no adjudication, supersedes, or exempts edge is declared for the ActiveComponents→settling placement specifically.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-legibility\"},{\"id\":\"f2\",\"text\":\"dc-3 grants matrix and verdict links only to a default-branch class:feature entry with a non-empty story field ('today's exact condition'), while parent dc-4 promises 'matrix and verdict additionally for features' without qualification. The ADJ-32/f3 adjudication dc-3 cites covers the still-drafting feature on a design branch (no built evidence to link); it does not cover the residual case of a default-branch feature whose story field is empty — such an entry loses the matrix/verdict links the parent promises, and no supersedes/exempts edge or adjudication is declared for that narrowing.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-legibility\"}]}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/home-status-glance@0ce3ef20dff08cd6a6a23b1701c81d691339fc4a], digest: sha256:de3110614890bf1c83011d47a7e198f0fe2c8b01206882181704d3e84f2f8712, integrity: sha256:8ffc971d466c327a521ea9abbfa03faee910fa285be01e0459ed765514af8db8 }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-f1** [no-conflict]: dc-2 places StatusGroupActiveComponents — still-ACTIVE components — into the 'settling' bucket, but parent dc-4 defines the trailing settling group as 'any remaining active statuses (closed awaiting archive, superseded)', an enumeration that names only wind-down statuses. Classifying a component that is still active and presumably still actionable under the bucket the parent frames as post-actionable residue is at best an undeclared gap-fill and at worst a contradiction of dc-4's actionable-first taxonomy. The cited adjudication ADJ-32 settled only zone-aware exclusion of archive-zone Terminal entries; no adjudication, supersedes, or exempts edge is declared for the ActiveComponents→settling placement specifically. (confidence 0.45) — Controller adjudication ADJ-36 (2026-07-16): parent dc-4's 'any remaining active statuses' is a TOTAL catch-all over the three-bucket partition's remainder (the author of the ADJ-24 taxonomy is the sitting adjudicator); the parenthetical '(closed awaiting archive, superseded)' illustrates the common wind-down cases and does not bound it. A still-active component is neither awaiting design/accept action nor in the build queue, so the trailing group is its only home; excluding it would make the glance a partial answer to parent dc-2's 'what state is everything in'. No conflict. The finding's semantic point is met at the presentation layer: the trailing group's display label may be worded honestly for steady-state entries at build discretion, within dc-5's fixed glance-group-settling testid (testids address, they do not dictate display text).
- **judged-f2** [rejected]: dc-3 grants matrix and verdict links only to a default-branch class:feature entry with a non-empty story field ('today's exact condition'), while parent dc-4 promises 'matrix and verdict additionally for features' without qualification. The ADJ-32/f3 adjudication dc-3 cites covers the still-drafting feature on a design branch (no built evidence to link); it does not cover the residual case of a default-branch feature whose story field is empty — such an entry loses the matrix/verdict links the parent promises, and no supersedes/exempts edge or adjudication is declared for that narrowing. (confidence 0.40) — Controller adjudication ADJ-36 (2026-07-16) confirms rejected per ADJ-32: the original f3 explicitly enumerated 'a default-branch feature with no stories yet, gets a board link only' and ADJ-32 rejected f3 in full. A default-branch feature with an empty story field has no story ref to construct a /matrix or /verdict address — the link cannot be built, and dc-3 mirrors directory.go's existing condition (class is feature and the story field is non-empty; dc-1 consumed-not-re-derived, never a third grammar): a link that cannot work does not exist to give.
